issues.title: A way to distinguish TextEditingController event types.
issues.body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Use case

<!--
     Please tell us the problem you are running into that led to you wanting
     a new feature.

     Is your feature request related to a problem? Please give a clear and
     concise description of what the problem is.

     Describe the alternative solutions you've considered. Is there a package
     on pub.dev/flutter that already solves this?
-->

When using the `TextEditingController.addListener(myListener)` I get events from text changes but also from focus changes.
If I want to change my state, let's say showing an alert at some point when the user is typing, `myListener` is called 2 more times.
Meaning that I need to keep track of the previous text and check every time if the text did actually change or if it was just a focus event.
Same happens when the `TextField` get focused or unfocused. I get calls to `myListener`.

## Proposal

Having two listener types for `TextEditingController`. One for focus events and another one just for text changes.

<!--
     Briefly but precisely describe what you would like Flutter to be able to do.

     Consider attaching images showing what you are imagining.

     Does this have to be provided by Flutter directly, or can it be provided
     by a package on pub.dev/flutter? If so, maybe consider implementing and
     publishing such a package rather than filing a bug.
-->
comments: I believe that having two events is better than just removing the focus event because it would give us more control over the textfield. But I'm used to event driven platforms, and flutter isn't that event driven, so the dev team will know what's best.

I think one of the event is triggered by selection changes? The text editing value includes both selection and text (as well as composing). When a textfield gets focused, the selection changes from TextSelection.collapsed(offset: -1) to TextSelection.collapsed(offset: text.length)

Yeah I think the behavior of calling the listener for both selection and text changes is working as intended, so this is a proposal and not a bug.  [TextEditingValue](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/TextEditingValue-class.html) includes both selection and text as @chunhtai said, and since [TextEditingController](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/TextEditingController-class.html) is just a ValueNotifier<TextEditingValue>, it will call the listener whenever the TextEditingValue changes at all.

The workaround mentioned in the description above: https://dartpad.dartlang.org/eec5ab37140ce52c8eb530b861960907

I wonder if it would be possible to write a subclass of TextEditingController that has `addTextListener` and `addSelectionListener`?
Just a FYI, there is a onChange parameter for textfield that is specifically for text changes.
IIRC when `EditableText` gets unfocused it sets the selection to `(-1, -1)`. It's probably not needed anymore. Let me see if I can remove it.
@LongCatIsLooong Be aware the desktop accessibility uses -1 to indicate there is no valid selection.

> Yeah I think the behavior of calling the listener for both selection and text changes is working as intended, so this is a proposal and not a bug. [TextEditingValue](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/TextEditingValue-class.html) includes both selection and text as @chunhtai said, and since [TextEditingController](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/TextEditingController-class.html) is just a ValueNotifier, it will call the listener whenever the TextEditingValue changes at all.
> 
> The workaround mentioned in the description above: https://dartpad.dartlang.org/eec5ab37140ce52c8eb530b861960907
> 
> I wonder if it would be possible to write a subclass of TextEditingController that has `addTextListener` and `addSelectionListener`?

Or maybe add a type parameter to the event?

> Just a FYI, there is a onChange parameter for textfield that is specifically for text changes.

Yeah but it's triggered only by human interaction while the TextEditingController event is triggered also when the text is changed programatically. So the onChange doesn't fit all scenarios.

> Having two listener types for TextEditingController. One for focus events and another one just for text changes.

It is already possible. You can check in your `TextEditingValue` listener if the text field's `FocusNode` has focus: https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/FocusNode/hasFocus.html

> > Having two listener types for TextEditingController. One for focus events and another one just for text changes.
> 
> It is already possible. You can check in your `TextEditingValue` listener if the text field's `FocusNode` has focus: https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/FocusNode/hasFocus.html

Well yes, but it's more of a workaround. It doesn't work for all scenarios.

I made the focus change problem into a different issue #86752. 

So the other part of the proposal is that `ValueListenable<T>` needs to be able to accept `void Function(T before, T after)` as listeners, so you can filter out uninteresting changes?

> I made the focus change problem into a different issue #86752.
> 
> So the other part of the proposal is that `ValueListenable<T>` needs to be able to accept `void Function(T before, T after)` as listeners, so you can filter out uninteresting changes?

It would make it a little easier to check if the text actually changed. But it will still require our management to check `if(after != before)`.

My suggestion is that the event is triggered only `if(after != before)`.
Unassigning since the issue I originally intended to address  (#86752) has been addressed.
This issue is missing a priority label. Please set a priority label when adding the `triaged-framework` label.

What is the correct behavior ? no spaces for both? or one space for both?

The real cause is the cursor width , it is not a character space as it seem , the documentation says that negative values can be used to reverse this effect ,,you can use cursorWidth = -2  , unfortunately, this may work in release build only as there is an assert preventing it to accept negative values , I will make PR to correct this behavior .

Thanks for looking into this. 
Since the correct behaviour is no space between hint text and suffix text, is there a way to actually achieve a space?

I tried adding a space at the end of the suffix text but trailing spaces do not seem to have an effect or they get trimmed off somewhere. Or is there a unicode "hard space" that can be used at the end of the suffix text to force a space?

> I tried adding a space at the end of the suffix text but trailing spaces do not seem to have an effect or they get trimmed off somewhere. Or is there a unicode "hard space" that can be used at the end of the suffix text to force a space?

`'\u202f'` seems to do the trick.

@Reekoooo started work on this in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/93634. If anyone would like to take that work across the finishing line please don't hesitate to take the code from that PR and apply the review comments @justinmc left there, and then create a new PR with the result!

comments:
Hi @fufesou 
Can `Option + Control` be equivalent to `AltGR`? (Per https://support.apple.com/en-vn/102221)

I checked this using `keychron k1 bluetooth` keyboard which has `Option + Control` on the right and here is the result:

![Screenshot 2024-08-21 at 19 01 43](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/15a6fd4b-b406-49cf-acaa-a6948b0b9954)

Can you also verify this on the latest Flutter stable/master channel? Thanks!
> Can `Option` + `Control` be equivalent to `AltGR`? (Per https://support.apple.com/en-vn/102221)

No. I've tried `Option/Alt Left` + `Control Left` and  `Option/Alt Right` + `Control Left`.

And your screenshot also did not generate `AltGr`.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/fbed99fd4bc43bea899c4de39240badb82235809/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/keyboard_key.g.dart#L732

> Can you also verify this on the latest Flutter stable/master channel? Thanks!

I've tried flutter `3.24.0`, same issue.

I just want to make the steps to be more clearly. Can you confirm the steps below?

1. Run the example code on macOS
2.  In case it's US keyboard layout, press `Option/Alt Left` + `Control Left` or `Option/Alt Right` + `Control Left`. In case it's Germany keyboard layout, press `AltGR` key.

- Actual result: it detects separate key events (`Option/Alt Left` + `Control Left` or `Option/Alt Right` + `Control Left`) instead of `AltGR` key event
- Expected result: able to detect `AltGR` key event

I don't have a keyboard that has AltGR key so I can't try this. Can you also confirm this on Web app?




I've tested. Still the same issue. Both macOS and Web app.

BTW, I don't think you need to have a keyboard that has `AltGR`.

I just change the input source to German. The `Alt/Option Right` should be `AltGr`.



Thanks for the update. I changed the input source to German and saw `Alt Right` only as well. Checked on Flutter stable `3.24.1` and master `3.25.0-1.0.pre.102`.

Do you mean somehow embedding native webview instead of webview_flutter?

No, I meant using any native Android webview, maybe try https://www.digitalocean.com/community/tutorials/android-webview-example-tutorial and see if using it, you get same behavior as with flutter ?

But the issue is caused by using webview together with TextField. Not sure I can reproduce the same app as above on pure Android.

Ok, thanks for the update. I tried again with a native app and observed that, if we disable virtual keyboard, then type in a native emulator app (ex: messages or notes), then it does take the input and then if you run your code sample, focus on webview textfield, it does take the input at first attempt, as shown in below video:


https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/67046386/ed5c297c-4d8e-4cab-8629-5a193f432007


But after you have inputed once, then going back to it and trying to input, then it doesn't take the input. 
Can you try the same and see if it helps in your case ?

Yes, the problem is as you described.
Also, the issue with physical keyboard doesn't depend on whether virtual keyboard is disabled or not. However, even when physical keyboard isn't able to take an input, virtual still works fine.
Thanks for the update. Per above / earlier verification and based on the report, keeping it open for team's attention.
@mariamhas for priority review, potentially related to large format device support.

Hi everyone, just wanted to know if there's been any progress on this issue.
Also, in the meantime, do you have any ideas about a potential workaround?

Hi, I have been experiencing the same issue and wanted to provide a little additional context (but lmk if this constitutes a separate bug report / discussion) -- there seems to be an underlying issue with focus here, such that when the text widget has focus and you click on the webview input, the caret remains in the text widget and it stays in its focused state. Clicking out of the webview back to the text widget does not necessarily remove focus from the webview text input element itself but does trigger a window blur event, so the caret disappears there. 

As mentioned previously, when using the virtual keyboard this issue does not occur, however the focus issue is still present (text widget remains in a highlighted state with caret while you are inputing into webview input, so there are two inputs on screen with carets which should never be the case, even if text input is correct).
Same issue.

issues.title: Keyboard Resets on emoji input on Android OPPO Find X5 Pro
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a simple app with TextField
2. Run app on OPPO Find X5 Pro
3. When inputting an emoji, observe how keyboard resets back annyoingly from emoji selection to alphabetical
4. The problem can be reproduced even with a stateful widget and adding a textediting controller

### Expected results

 The keyboard should not reset back to normal state when inputting an emoji
 


### Actual results

The keyboard resets back on every emoji selection (even when deleting the emoji from the delete-icon in Emoji Selection Tab)

Furthermore, the blob that appears when selecting a text (long-pressing the textfield), that can be seen in the videos, is under the keyboard

### Code sample

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MainApp());
}

class MainApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MainApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const MaterialApp(
      home: KeyboardTest(),
    );
  }
}

class KeyboardTest extends StatelessWidget {
  const KeyboardTest({
    super.key,
  });

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(

}

issues.title: [proposal] ability to change text overflow on the TextField
issues.body:
If the user inputs more text than can fit into the available space of a single line `TextFormField`, then end of the text is simply cropped. 
In debug mode, an overflow warning is also displayed on screen.

Unlike the `Text` widget however, the `TextFormField` does not have an `overflow` parameter so that we can for example show an ellipsis in this situation.

Implementing `overflow` handling for the `TextFormField` will allow us to give valuable visual feedback to users.

Hi there, this workaround helped me a lot:

```
Size measureTextSize(
      String text,
      TextStyle style, {
      int maxLines = 1,
      double maxWidth = double.infinity,
    }) {
      final TextPainter textPainter = TextPainter(
        text: TextSpan(text: text, style: style),
        textDirection: TextDirection.ltr,
        maxLines: maxLines,
      )..layout(minWidth: 0, maxWidth: maxWidth);

      return textPainter.size;
    }
```

issues.title: Support iOS 18 formatting menu
issues.body:
### Use case At WWDC 2024, Apple [announced](https://youtu.be/wIJrVz5NGHY?t=975) a new text input formatting menu.
Opened via the context menu Format button
### Proposal Flutter may want to support this for rich text editors. It's not yet clear to me if there is a sufficient native API for showing the menu and receiving any button presses. CC @MitchellGoodwin for Cupertino. CC @Renzo-Olivares for rich text. CC @LouiseHsu who added various other context menu buttons like [Share](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/132599). CC @matthew-carroll, I wonder if this is relevant to super_editor.
comments:
@justinmc we've already had someone ask us about Super Editor support for pretty much everything related to text editing from WWDC.

Going back to many previous discussions that we've had on the topic - the community can't replace the text input system in Flutter. Anything Flutter doesn't support from the OS is a prohibitive restriction on all Flutter apps and packages. Therefore, unless there's a very unusual situation, any and all IME related behaviors from the OS should be made available within Flutter. I don't see why these new capabilities from WWDC would be any different.

Would love to see this.

One big problem will be font-family and colors.  

For rich text editors we use to generate content for the web or cross-platforms the Apple font family choices (either as a limit or not limiting enough) can be a significant problem.  So hopefully the implementation would allow us to either restrict or pipe in our own font family selections and color choice options

At the very least we should be able to turn off the system level formatting as well so it would not conflict with editors such as super editor so should we want that
Sounds good. Someone will need to look at what the native APIs look like specifically, but I'm in favor of Flutter exposing those more or less directly for people that are building rich text editors.

Work on FFIgen is ongoing but I'm not sure if there's anything being done right now to use it to replace the existing platform channels, if that's what you mean. Reworking the IME/framework communication is also not being worked on as far as I know.

@justinmc if that's the case, how do you intend to mirror the platform APIs in the framework, as you recently mentioned you thought you should? Are you going to manually add all the many missing APIs by hand to the method channels?

I think I mentioned that in relation to scribe (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/148784), where I'm planning to expose Android's scribe APIs more or less verbatim. That's as far as my planning goes right now, but if anyone else wants to rethink those connections I'm happy to review.

@justinmc can you elaborate on why the IME area moves piece by piece, seemingly with conflicting approaches? I think at this point it's been demonstrated over a period of years that this isn't working out very well. But as has been the issue all along, this is an area that is 100% owned by the Flutter framework. There's nothing any of the rest of us can do about it outside. 

At the moment, given the long history with the two of us, and @Renzo-Olivares, I don't really know what the official perspective is of the Flutter team at this point. What's the plan for the framework's overall IME integration? Why is that the plan? Who's working on what? This is all murky and it makes it more or less impossible for us on the outside to interface with those decisions or to validate the approaches. Yet those decisions and plans are absolutely critical to a whole array of packages and Flutter products. What can we do about this?

I don't know of any plan that you'd be interfering with if you wanted to write a proposal or a proof of concept or something. Are you worried about advances in FFIgen superseding any work on the IME/framework interface? Maybe I could try to dig up some info on that but I currently know of no plans to change anything there.

@Renzo-Olivares previously said he was investigating multiple options for generating the APIs. Then I never heard anything about it again. You've mentioned that you seem to agree that Flutter shouldn't hide APIs, but then there doesn't seem to be any plan to make that happen. The issue, again, is that it's totally unclear what you guys are doing, not doing, why, and when. In the absence of basic transparency there, I'm certainly not going to spend a bunch of time writing a proposal, nor would I expect anyone else to do that either. The first step is for the Flutter team to clearly state where it is, and where it thinks it's going, and only then would it make any sense for an outside contributor to invest the effort to explore technical approaches are draft some kind of proposal.

It would be great to publish something like that. I'll look into it.

In the meantime if you ever want me to test the waters for you if you are thinking about trying to make a bigger change, feel free to reach out. I can definitely ask around at Google and try to find anyone with conflicting ideas to avoid you wasting time.

@justinmc I've been waiting this whole time for you guys to publish your intentions for generating bindings for the IME because, like I said, Renzo stated he was working on it a long time ago. So if that effort has silently disappeared (bad communication), then yes the community will need to look into this. Can you please verify across the team that no one is doing anything conflicting in that area, and verify that there aren't any known blockers related to FFI and JNI gen for such a purpose?

Moreover, where does the community need to plug into the current team operations to ensure that this isn't a big wasted effort. This will take a lot of research and analysis, so if this effort turns into a situation that looks like the Flutter team isn't engaging in good faith, and isn't considering the need to get a solution integrated, then it will probably be my last attempt to ever do so.
Let me create a separate issue so we have one place to concentrate our efforts and I'll post right back. If there's already one let me know.
@justinmc I'm not aware of one.

Here it is: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/150460

Let me know if I've butchered what you're thinking or if you can elaborate anywhere. Feel free to edit the issue yourself.

issues.title: Disable the input action button in the virtual keyboard when TextField is empty
issues.body: ## Use case I'd like to disable the search button in the virtual keyboard when the TextField is empty: ## Proposal On iOS, this can be implemented via [enablesReturnKeyAutomatically](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uitextinputtraits/1624432-enablesreturnkeyautomatically?language=objc).
comments:
Hi @ryanheise, thanks for filing the issue. 
I see there are some similar open issues addressing the case you described #93915, #30331
Closing this issue as a duplicate, so please follow up on these issues for further updates. You can leave there your comment and react an upvote to the original comment will help to raise the issue. Thank you!

This feature request is unrelated to #30331 because changing the button label dynamically is a different goal from disabling the button.

As for #93915 , that issue has a different and broader objective of being able to dynamically disable/enable the button at any time, which I'm not sure is even permitted by iOS. Rather, the native iOS API only allows you to follow a single preset behaviour of disabling the button when the text field is empty, and then automatically enabling it when you start typing. See the iOS documentation linked above. My feature request is specifically for that feature.

If I'm wrong, please let me know (i.e. which native API makes #93915 possible). But if #93915 is not actually possible, this present issue still is possible. If we close that issue, then this one should remain open.

Hi @ryanheise 
The reason I bring up the above 2 issues is that I am not sure if a feature when implemented will combine automation and disable/enable together.

With what you described above, I think it matches what the OP of issue #93915 is describing, please see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/93915#issuecomment-974593072. It would be better if similar issues were solved in only one issue, so please leave a comment there for your thoughts and react an upvote to the original comment will help to raise the issue. It will make that issue clearer when implemented.

If you disagree, please write in the comments and I will reopen this issue and continue the discussion. Thanks!
As before, I don't think #93915 is actually possible. Please correct me if I'm wrong.

Hi @ryanheise I reopen the issue for further discussions.

Why do you think that #93915 is impossible when you are also proposing the similar thing?
- From your proposal:
>I'd like to disable the search button in the virtual keyboard when the TextField is empty:
- From #93915:
>The app is Safari,The Go button is disabled if you enter nothing in the search bar.

---

>please let me know (i.e. which native API makes https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/93915 possible)

Also, this is not known in advance, that's the reason why I suggested you should add your comment on that issue so people can see it and give more opinions on it before being implemented (e.g. using [enablesReturnKeyAutomatically](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uitextinputtraits/1624432-enablesreturnkeyautomatically?language=objc) as you suggested)

It sounds like #93915 has proposed a more general feature. If you could have an option to enable/disable the TextField, that would also solve my use case:

```dart
TextField(enableInputAction: true, ...);
```

But it would also solve a broader range of use cases. For example, with that ability, you would also be able to perform validation after each keystroke and disable the action button until the input is valid. You could decide on the condition for when to enable/disable the button, including when the text field is empty. Having such a feature would be nice.

But I'm just proposing a feature that is more narrow:

```dart
TextField(autoEnableReturnKey: true, ...);
```

Basically, I want to expose a native feature of the platform directly.

I assumed it would be best to have a distinct issue for each feature proposal, rather than a distinct issue for each use case, since some feature proposals will solve a broad range of use cases. But I'm fine if you want to handle everything on the other issue. I'll add a comment there.

Using [`enablesReturnKeyAutomatically`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uitextinputtraits/1624432-enablesreturnkeyautomatically?language=objc) probably works, but it might be more flexible to pass a closure like this: 

```
TextField(
  enableKeyboardReturnKey: (String text) => text.isNotEmpty, 
  ...
)
```

This gives developers full control (e.g. phone number format validation).

What you're describing is what is already covered by the other issue #93915 so if you like that idea, you should vote for that other issue. Note that using a closure doesn't make any difference one way or the other. You do not need a closure to achieve that because you can simply rebuild the widget tree with a different parameter value as needed.

My proposal here is a different proposal based on just exposing the direct capability of the platform. There's not much we can do about that, that's what the platform supports, so that's what I'm proposing we expose in the API.

> Note that using a closure doesn't make any difference one way or the other. You do not need a closure to achieve that because you can simply rebuild the widget tree with a different parameter value as needed.

The keyboard is a separate window that's not part of the Flutter widget tree, so we may have to use a closure here, though.
That's true, although with the native API you still don't get any choice about when to disable the button except when the text field is empty so the closure still doesn't help.

The display effect is normal before the verification trigger, as shown below：

![IMG_20221109_120419](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36031579/200736642-17e585cc-5cdc-4f96-8950-5a1ac0fba43b.jpg)

But after the verification trigger, the display effect is not normal, as follows：

![IMG_20221109_120332](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36031579/200736747-465e13e0-baf5-4425-a214-3f133f5a44d6.jpg)
comments:
I agree with @carmel that the behavior of [TextFormField](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextFormField-class.html) is annoying to say the least
I have the feeling that we are in the territory of "feels like a bug, but work as intended"

I would like to add on top of what has been already said
that a very common use case is to put the text field inside a fixed height widget (like a [Card](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/Card-class.html)) 
or wanting it otherwise fixed in place

unfortunately the behavior is that the text field grows
overlapping other widgets or causing them to overflow ([code sample](https://dartpad.dartlang.org/?id=f28393fcb209ec1c28f8839c53ffdc0d)),
that is why I sympathize with @carmel 's approach of providing constraints to the widget.

a quick fix could be 
1. add explicit and modifiable default constraints (so that the widget won't "grow")
2. move to move the constraints in the constructor, so that those could be used in the ui

eg:
```dart
(context) {
    final textField = MyTextField();
    return Container(
      height: textField.contraints.maxHeight + 48,
      child: ...
    );
  })

```

@iapicca  Thanks for your reply, maybe I still can't reduce the height to a satisfactory value the way you did. I tried doing it the way described [here](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/15400#issuecomment-475773473) and found that the height shrinks a little bit after verification triggered.

This issue causes even bigger problems on web since mobile and web TextFormFields have different heights on the 2 platforms. So we can't apply the Figma designs pixel perfect. If there is no error texts, we can somehow manually adjust them with SizedBox and expands:true but as I said, this does not work exactly same in web canvasKit.

Please we need a simple way to set a height to a TextFormField. Not workarounds. If you are afraid of breaking current apps, please create a new Widget called MaterialTextFormField and write it from scratch. This is one of the most basic needs of an app.
its 2024 still this issue exist, has anyone found any workaround for this??

Since https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/80754, it is possible to use `InputDecoration.constraints` to constraint a `TextField` (for instance to set a fixed height).

The provided code sample uses this parameter to set a fixed height of 38 pixels which is enough to render the input field container. Unfortunately this is not enough to render the error text (which is part of the `TextField`).

When not relying on `InputDecoration.constraints`, the default behavior is to increase the `TextField` height in order to display the error text. If this is not wanted, setting the `helperText` to `""` will reserve a space under the input container where the error message will be displayed (helper, counter and error are displayed in the same area).

When relying on `InputDecoration.constraints`, it is up to the developper to set the right constraints (choosing to set a fixed height, choosing to set a minHeight and a different maxHeight, etc). As shown in the provided code sample, this might lead to setting values that break the rendering. Throwing an error in that case might be a solution? Improving the documentation for `InputDecoration.constraints` will probably be a less intrusive solution.

Providing default values as suggested in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/114952#issuecomment-1308330743 is not possible because many parameters are in play to determine this value (various text styles, contentPadding, isDense, isCollapsed, etc).

About the height difference between web and mobile, mentioned in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/114952#issuecomment-1627829871, this is related to visual density (which defaults to `VisualDensity.compact` on desktop/web and `VisualDensity.standard` on mobile). The default value can be overridden using `ThemeData.visualDensity`, for instance

```dart
    return  MaterialApp(
      theme: ThemeData(visualDensity: VisualDensity.standard),
``` 

Adding examples in the documentation to illustrate some of those use cases is probably the best way to make progress on this issue.
This issue is missing a priority label. Please set a priority label when adding the `triaged-design` label.

issues.title: iOS "Scribble" feature: cannot tap on text selection controls with Apple Pencil
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Add a `TextField` or a `CupertinoTextField` to the application (see sample code below).
2. Add some text using the [scribble](https://support.apple.com/guide/ipad/enter-text-with-scribble-ipad355ab2a7/ipados) feature with the Apple Pencil.
3. Select some of the text.
4. Now try to hit a button on the text selection controls (e.g. "copy") **using the Apple Pencil**. 

### Expected results

As in other iOS apps, it should be possible to copy, cut, etc. by hitting the corresponding button with the **Apple Pencil**.

### Actual results

The button is not hit; instead, the input seems to be directed to the underlying canvas. (See attached video.)
If the buttons are tapped with the finger, though, they work just fine.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/cupertino.dart';
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MainApp());
}

class MainApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MainApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Center(
          child: ConstrainedBox(
            constraints: const BoxConstraints(maxWidth: 450),
            child: CupertinoTextField(
              scribbleEnabled: true,
              selectionControls: cupertinoTextSelectionControls,
            ),
          ),

issues.title: SearchAnchor View pops unexpectedly while using Web version in Desktop mode on Android
comments:
@Roonil Thanks for the report. Seeing the same behaviour with latest SDK versions.

```
stable : 3.38.5
master : 3.40.0-1.0.pre-311
```

Acknowledging the problem, but to set expectations, if this requires significant work, I do not expect the core team to pick it up any time soon. Adaptations between desktop and mobile form factors work differently in Flutter. Requesting the desktop site at the browser level changes the user agent string, content scaling, and other unknown amount of behaviors. Instead, the expectation is that the app can scale its content between desktop and mobile using the adaptive features of the framework. Flutter supports building apps for both mobile and desktop screens from the same codebase. I'd recommend leaning on this when designing Flutter apps across multiple form factors (phones, tablets, desktop).

> Acknowledging the problem, but to set expectations, if this requires significant work, I do not expect the core team to pick it up any time soon. Adaptations between desktop and mobile form factors work differently in Flutter. Requesting the desktop site at the browser level changes the user agent string, content scaling, and other unknown amount of behaviors. Instead, the expectation is that the app can scale its content between desktop and mobile using the adaptive features of the framework. Flutter supports building apps for both mobile and desktop screens from the same codebase. I'd recommend leaning on this when designing Flutter apps across multiple form factors (phones, tablets, desktop).

I agree. But, as it is a website and the end-user has the full flexibility over the "mode" he chooses (Desktop and Mobile), it'll be very unexpected to see things working correctly in one scenario but not in the other, when (in theory) both should be functionally equivalent. Again, this is a web application and so the flexibility that web application provides (working regardless of the user-agent string) should indeed work here as well.

issues.title: [bug] TextInput cursor positioned over hintText
issues.body: When using a hintText in an InputDecoration for a TextField, the cursor and the hintText are too close. Yeah, I did that, but I found no issue to track this problem, so I created this one.

issues.title: TextInputAction.next on Flutter Web doesn't always select full text in the next field (inconsistent focus behavior)
issues.body:
## Update

This issue was caused by the `_justResumed` flag being stored **per `EditableTextState`**, which was set to `true` on app resume for every `TextField`, but only reset when each individual field was focused again. This led to unreliable behavior where `TextInputAction.next`, `tab`, or programmatic focus would **not auto-select text** as expected on Web and Desktop after resume.

### Steps to reproduce

On Flutter Web, when using `TextInputAction.next` and pressing the Enter key, the focus correctly moves to the next `TextField` via:
```dart
bool nextFocus() => FocusTraversalGroup.of(context!).next(this);
```
However, sometimes the text in the newly focused field is not fully selected. Instead, the cursor is placed at the end of the existing text. This is inconsistent behavior, as the full text should ideally be selected to allow for easy replacement.

This issue seems to occur intermittently, and I haven't been able to reliably reproduce it.

This used to work  correctly in flutter version `3.10.6`

### Expected results

When the user presses Enter (triggering TextInputAction.next), the focus should move to the next field and the entire text in that field should be selected automatically.

### Actual results

- Focus does move to the next field.
- However, sometimes:
    - The text is not selected.
    - The cursor is placed at the end of the text instead.

### Code sample

write something in both fields. move focus to first field and press `enter` to trigger.
This issue seems to occur intermittently, and I haven't been able to reliably reproduce it.

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  State<MyApp> createState() => _MyAppState();
}

class _MyAppState extends State<MyApp> {

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Padding(
          padding: const EdgeInsets.all(8.0),
          child: Row(
            children: [
              Expanded(
                child: TextFormField(
                  decoration: InputDecoration(hintText: "Field 1"),
                  textInputAction: TextInputAction.next,
                ),
              ),
              const SizedBox(width: 10,),
              Expanded(
                child: TextFormField(
                  decoration: InputDecoration(hintText: "Field 1"),
                  textInputAction: TextInputAction.next,
                ),
              ),
            ],
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

issues.title: TextEditingController.text docs miss a reason
issues.body:
`TextEditingController.text` docs say it shouldn't be updated in a `build` method, but it doesn't explain why, it just says the listeners will be called. Is this for performance reasons, so the listeners won't lock the `build` method? Or are there any other issues as well?

I think the docs should include a more explained reason on the side-effects, so devs could evaluate more the impacts of their code and their decisions, or facilitate fixing bugs.
comments:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/78951b0c01b7f5abc072974981d62830709dd9f1/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L137

I think setting the property instead of using the constructor parameter would look very non-idiomatic.  It sounds like they did attempt to explain why someone shouldn't do it.  Since you found it confusing maybe you can suggest a fix @feinstein?

@gaaclarke the `text` property in `TextEditingController` is for changing the `text` on the fly, like in a button. In this case, we don't want to rebuild a new `TextEditingController`, so the constructor parameter isn't helpful and we need a separate property for this.

I can't suggest an fix as I don't know why they suggested this. For example, if the recommendation is only about performance issues, then it should say something as :

> .... as we can't know how many listeners are there and since they will be called synchronously, they can hang the build process leading to decreased performance and visible lag in animations.

But if the reason is something else entirely, like calling internal methods of the framework that shouldn't be called inside the `build` method, then I don't know, and only they can explain the reason.

That's correct for a `StatelessWidget` but with `StatefulWidgets` things aren't so simple, as your state can be defined in multiple places, callbacks, that's why the Flutter framework has several controllers, like `TextEditingController`, `AnimationController`, `PageController`, as explained [here](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/53667793/what-are-controllers-in-flutter).

What you are implying is that the Flutter framework creators are wrong in exposing the `text` property, and not only this property, but all the other ones from several other controllers. This controller pattern is common in Flutter in general and is very useful for streamlining reactive programming, otherwise you will have to create several variables and update them in callbacks, which gets messy very quickly.
@Hixie could you clarify this please?

Still valid since the  actual documentation does not provide more details about the side effects of updating the text property during build phase.

https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/TextEditingController/text.html

The reason to not set `text` during build is what the docs say -- it would cause listeners to fire, and you don't want listeners during during build because they might call setState, or do other things, that would invalidate the current build. In general, the build phase should have no business logic at all -- it should have no side effects, the build method is just a function that maps current state to widgets.

@Hixie this is a very general recommendation for Flutter that I see often, is it written on any docs so we can link it for the `text`, for people (specially inexperienced) that want to learn more?

This way we keep the docs small and don't repeat it all over the place.
Hmm, I can't think of anything where we say that. We really should. We should add it to the State.build and StatelessWidget.build API docs, for sure...
Agreed. Do you want me to open an issue about it, or do we track it here?
We can fix it as part of this issue.

This issue is reproducible on the latest `stable` and `master` channels.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/78951b0c01b7f5abc072974981d62830709dd9f1/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L137-L140

https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/TextEditingController/text.html

issues.title: TextField suffixText position cannot be changed
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Try to display suffix text right after the input value instead of the right corner
2. As a solution, override buildTextSpan to include suffix text as suffix text
3. Get "RangeError (end): Invalid value: Not in inclusive range" error when suffix text is included into selection:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/036b52e7-a451-4cb5-acd2-e26a57833b76

### Expected results

Expected result is a text field with suffix text that is right after the typed value:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/883d0c7a-8064-4a50-9dcf-4018f4f0be28

To achieve the desired result, I'd expect in InputDecoration a parameter called suffixTextPosition that would enable to show suffix text right after the typed value.

### Actual results

Currently suffixText is only displayed on the right corner:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0dad4964-6df8-41d1-90f7-b1934ced8c53

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const Scaffold(
      body: TextField(
        controller: InputController(suffixText: '₸'),
        decoration: InputDecoration(
          // There is no way to change its position.
          suffixText: '₸',
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

class InputController extends TextEditingController {
  final String? suffixText;
  final FocusNode? focusNode;

  InputController({
    super.text,
    this.suffixText,
    this.focusNode,
  });

  bool get _hasFocus {
    return focusNode?.hasFocus ?? false;
  }

  @override
  TextSpan buildTextSpan({
    required BuildContext context,
    TextStyle? style,
    required bool withComposing,
  }) {
    return TextSpan(
      style: style,
      children: [
        TextSpan(
          text: value.text,
          style: style,
        ),
        if (suffixText != null) ...[
          if (_hasFocus || value.text.isNotEmpty)
            TextSpan(
              text: ' $suffixText',
              style: style,
            ),
        ]
      ],
    );
  }
}
```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video



As a solution, buildTextSpan of TextEditingController was overridden to include text span that imitates suffix text. Since value must not include suffix text, the following error is thrown when the suffix text is included into selection:

issues.title: KeyEvent doesn't expose modifier flags from NSEvent on macOS, breaking synthetic keyboard events from external apps
issues.body:
### Title
KeyEvent doesn't expose modifier flags from NSEvent on macOS, breaking synthetic keyboard events from external apps

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a Flutter macOS app with keyboard event handling
2. Use an external app (like Raycast, Alfred, or any app) that sends synthetic keyboard events with modifier flags (e.g., Cmd+V to paste)
3. Add logging in both Swift (NSEvent level) and Dart (KeyEvent level) to observe modifier flags
4. Trigger a synthetic keyboard event with modifiers (e.g., use Raycast's emoji picker and select an emoji, which sends Cmd+V)
5. Observe that the NSEvent has correct modifierFlags, but Flutter's KeyEvent doesn't expose them
6. HardwareKeyboard.instance.isMetaPressed returns false because no physical Command key was pressed

### Expected results

Flutter's KeyEvent should expose the modifier flags that are present in the underlying NSEvent, independent of HardwareKeyboard state. When an NSEvent arrives with modifierFlags containing .command (0x100000), the corresponding Flutter KeyEvent should provide a way to access this information.

Expected API would be something like:
- event.hasCommandModifier (boolean)
- event.modifierFlags (set or int)
- Or at minimum, have HardwareKeyboard automatically sync with NSEvent modifiers

This would allow apps to properly handle synthetic keyboard events from external applications (Raycast, Alfred, Keyboard Maestro, etc.) that correctly set NSEvent.modifierFlags but don't send separate modifier key down/up events.

### Actual results

Flutter's KeyEvent only exposes modifiers through HardwareKeyboard.instance, which tracks physical keyboard state separately from individual event modifiers. When a synthetic NSEvent arrives with modifier flags set, this information is lost because:

1. The NSEvent has modifierFlags: 0x100000 (Command) - confirmed via Swift logging
2. Flutter creates a KeyEvent from this NSEvent
3. The KeyEvent doesn't expose the NSEvent's modifier flags
4. HardwareKeyboard.instance.isMetaPressed is false (because no physical key was pressed)
5. The app sees the event as just "V" instead of "Cmd+V"

This breaks compatibility with many macOS automation and productivity tools that send synthetic keyboard events.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/services.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: KeyboardListener(
          focusNode: FocusNode()..requestFocus(),
          autofocus: true,
          onKeyEvent: (KeyEvent event) {
            if (event is KeyDownEvent) {
              print('KeyEvent: ${event.logicalKey.keyLabel}');
              print('Character: ${event.character}');
              print('HardwareKeyboard.isMetaPressed: ${HardwareKeyboard.instance.isMetaPressed}');
              print('HardwareKeyboard.isControlPressed: ${HardwareKeyboard.instance.isControlPressed}');
              print('---');
              
              // BUG: Even if NSEvent has Command modifier flag set,
              // there's no way to access it from the KeyEvent
              // HardwareKeyboard only reflects physical key state
            }
          },
          child: const Center(
            child: Text(
              'Press keys or trigger synthetic events\n'
              'Try using Raycast emoji picker (Ctrl+Cmd+.) and selecting an emoji\n'
              'It sends Cmd+V but Flutter sees it as just "V"',
              textAlign: TextAlign.center,
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

**Swift code to observe the issue (add to MainFlutterWindow.swift):**

```swift
override func sendEvent(_ event: NSEvent) {
  if event.type == .keyDown {
    let eventModifiers = event.modifierFlags
    let systemModifiers = NSEvent.modifierFlags
    
    print("🔵 Swift: key=\(event.charactersIgnoringModifiers ?? ""), " +
          "event.modifierFlags=\(eventModifiers.rawValue), " +
          "NSEvent.modifierFlags=\(systemModifiers.rawValue)")
    
    // You'll see event.modifierFlags contains 0x100000 (Command)
    // but Flutter's KeyEvent won't expose this
  }
  super.sendEvent(event)
}
```

</details>

### Screenshots or Video

<details open>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

Console output showing the discrepancy:

Swift logs (NSEvent level):
```
🔵 Swift: key=v, event.modifierFlags=1048576, NSEvent.modifierFlags=0
```

Flutter logs (KeyEvent level):
```
KeyEvent: V
Character: v
HardwareKeyboard.isMetaPressed: false
HardwareKeyboard.isControlPressed: false
```

The NSEvent clearly has Command modifier (1048576 = 0x100000) but Flutter has no way to access it.

</details>

### Logs

<details open><summary>Logs</summary>

```console
🔵 Swift NSEvent: key=v, event.modifierFlags=1048576, NSEvent.modifierFlags=0
Flutter: KeyEvent: V
Flutter: Character: v  
Flutter: HardwareKeyboard.isMetaPressed: false
Flutter: Expected: Cmd+V paste action
Flutter: Actual: Just "V" character insertion

The modifier information exists in the NSEvent (1048576 = Command flag) but is not accessible from Flutter's KeyEvent API.
```

</details>

### Flutter Doctor output

<details open><summary>Doctor output</summary>

```console
[Run 'flutter doctor -v' and paste the output here]
```

</details>

---

### Additional context

This issue affects any macOS app that needs to integrate with:
- Raycast (emoji picker, clipboard manager)
- Alfred (workflows and snippets)
- Keyboard Maestro (macro automation)
- TextExpander (text expansion)
- Any other productivity tool that sends synthetic keyboard events

These tools correctly set NSEvent.modifierFlags but don't necessarily send separate modifier key press events, which is valid according to macOS/Cocoa conventions. Each NSEvent is meant to be self-contained with its own modifier state.

**Workaround:** Track modifier key timestamps and use a grace period to detect modifiers that were recently pressed. This is fragile and doesn't work when there's a delay between modifier release and character event (as with Raycast's emoji picker).

**Suggested fix:** Expose NSEvent.modifierFlags on KeyEvent, or at minimum have HardwareKeyboard sync its state from incoming NSEvent modifiers, not just from separate modifier key events.

issues.title: [SelectionArea] An incorrect context menu popped up when SelectionArea was nested
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

Right-clicking within a nested SelectionArea area may sometimes pop up the ContextMenu of the parent Selection.

### Expected results

Right-clicking within a nested SelectionArea area always pop up the ContextMenu of current SelectionArea.

### Actual results

Right-clicking within a nested SelectionArea area may sometimes pop up the ContextMenu of the parent Selection.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MainApp());
}

class MainApp extends StatefulWidget {
  const MainApp({super.key});

  @override
  State<MainApp> createState() => _MainAppState();
}

class _MainAppState extends State<MainApp> {

The `fyi-text-input` label is redundant with the `team-text-input` label.
The `triaged-design` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-design` label or `fyi-design` label.
Thanks for the triage! Happy to address any feedback from the Text Input team.

A note on the root cause of this issue: 

This issue occurs because of gesture conflicts between the nested regions since they both will use a `RawGestureDetector` internally with the same recognizers. In this case if a user holds their right click down for too long, nested selection area gesture detectors compete with each other, and the BaseTapGestureRecognizer exceeds its internal deadline and triggers the right click down callback indiscriminately. First the nested region triggers its menu, then the outer region triggers its menu. The reason sometimes the issue does not happen is because if the right click was fast enough the `GestureArena` will disambiguate between both gestures and declare a single winner.

issues.title: Code quality improvements for text input deltas
issues.body:
I'm creating an IME simulator in the flutter_test_robots package so that developers can simulate the kind of input that's used in document editing.

I found some code quality issues that made it difficult for me to get the simulator working. I recommend improving these details.

Please see:
 * Where Flutter handles delta JSON in the channel: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/text_input.dart#L1803
 * Where Flutter deserializes the delta JSON: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/text_editing_delta.dart#L65

Issues:
- **Provide a `toJSON()` for text editing deltas:** I'm sending deltas through the platform channel so that I get maximum correctness in the IME simulation. `TextEditingDelta` has a `fromJSON()` method, but no `toJSON()` method. Moreover, the `fromJSON()` method is very complicated. I found it impossible to read. So I made a guess about how I'm supposed to map values in the JSON blobs that I'm sending.
- **Clean up `TextEditingDelta.fromJSON()`:** As mentioned above, I couldn't visually trace what was happening in the `fromJSON()` method. It's doing a lot more than just de-serializing values and it's unclear what's happening. I recommend taking a look at some of Robert Martin's "clean code" guidelines to help improve the readability of that method.
- **Multiple areas fail silently:** I spent a while carefully tracing my fake JSON through the channel because any time something goes wrong in that channel, it fails silently. For example, I was passing some bad text bounds. This caused a "replace" call in `fromJSON()` to blow up, but I received no indication of this.
- **Conflicting terms for composing region:** In the JSON parsing there are references to the composing `start` and `end` values, and those are used interchangeably with `composingBase` and `composingExtent`. However, those are not the same concepts. As seen in `TextSelection`, `start` and `end` mean "upstream to downstream", but `base` and `extent` mean "a range in a user specified direction".
- **Use constants for message names and parameters:** All the message names are coded with inline strings. This *might* be appropriate for completely private details, but these names aren't private. These names are explicitly listed in the public docs for the channel. Thus, they should be public constants.
- **Offer message (de)serializers:** I mentioned individual delta serialization, but also in general there's no need to make other developers copy and paste the structure of messages. For example, the arguments for a delta message is a `List` that contains an `int`, followed by a `Map` that contains a `"deltas"` key, which then contains the `List` of deltas. Why not wrap that with a public object that others can use to access the data without worrying about the JSON structure?
- **Make it clear that the text input connection needs a special debug reset in tests:** I was hitting test failures with my simulator that I couldn't pin down. By luck, I found this line in the Flutter repo: `TextInputConnection.debugResetId();`. I'm still not sure why I need to call it. My widgets explicitly `close()` the input connection in their `dispose()` methods. Nonetheless, if I try to run more than 1 test at a time, the input connection holds on to the wrong connection ID and I can't connect the text fields in other tests.

You can see the simulator here: 
https://github.com/Flutter-Bounty-Hunters/flutter_test_robots/blob/main/lib/src/input_method_engine.dart

You can see how I'm testing the simulator here:
https://github.com/Flutter-Bounty-Hunters/flutter_test_robots/blob/main/test/flutter_test_robots_ime_test.dart
comments: CC @justinmc @Renzo-Olivares

Hi @matthew-carroll thanks for bringing up these concerns. 

- **Provide a toJSON() for text editing deltas:** This sounds like a reasonable addition to me. I'll look into adding this. 
- **Clean up TextEditingDelta.fromJSON():** I'll look into this along with general clarification on terms used in that method.
- **Multiple areas fail silently:** I plan on addressing these failures with #107426 . 
- **Conflicting terms for composing region:** I'll make sure to clarify this.
- **Use constants for message names and parameters:** This seems reasonable to me. Do you see any cons with this @justinmc ?

@matthew-carroll For constant message names and parameters, you mean things like [`TextInputClient.updateEditingStateWithDeltas`](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/3bb93dc86c06d9208ccee18088275114cd8256ec/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/text_input.dart#L1803) in the framework?  I guess because you want a future-proof way to listen to them yourself?

That seems to make sense, though it looks like all of our message names are done like this outside of deltas as well, so it would be a big change.  I guess the question is whether we do want to enable users to use these methods on their own.  If so then I'd be on board with migrating everything over to be public.

Yeah, that's what I mean.

Flutter should never have written any of these strings as literals. My guess is, someone started with a small piece and they threw something together with literals. Then everyone else after that just followed suite.

Flutter's channels are all inherently part of a public API, because any platform that integrates Flutter is expected to send messages over those channels. Similarly, any Flutter app should be able, if necessary, to install its own handler for those channels. For example, imagine a company that has an embedded system, so they port Flutter to their embedded system, and then they also build their app, which is meant to run on their embedded system. It would be silly to prevent that team from responding to their own platform messages.

I understand it's a big change. That's what happens when people make big mistakes. Nonetheless, I do think it's a mistake to pepper these literals around the code. And, as I mentioned above, the Dart Docs for many of these channels include the channel name and message names in the Dart Docs. Clearly these are values that developers are expected to know, and they're expected to be stable.
Thanks for making the case, I'm on board with that :+1:

This issue is assigned but has had no recent status updates. Please consider unassigning this issue if it is not going to be addressed in the near future. This allows people to have a clearer picture of what work is actually planned. Thanks!

issues.title: [Windows] Proposal to add missing TextField behaviours related to mouse clicks, autoscroll using mouse and with keyboard and combination keys.
issues.body:
### Consecutive mouse clicks for selection
- [working] - Double click to select the word
- [missing] - Triple click to select the paragraph
- [missing] - Four click to select the whole TextField

### Autoscroll while selection with mouse
- [missing] - While selecting and dragging the mouse, whenever mouse pointer goes beyond the lower boundary or upper boundary, TextField should autoscroll and selection should proceed further in respective direction. At present it is not possible to select the text with mouse which is beyond visible limit.

### Autoscroll while selection with keyboard
- [missing] - While selecting and proceeding further with Shift key + Arrow keys, if selection goes beyond visible limit TextField should scroll. At present textselection is proceeding but TextField does not scrolling in respective direction.

### Combination keys
**- [working] - Home key -**  to put cursor to line start
**- [missing] - Control key + Home key -** to put cursor to start of the TextField and scroll to it
**- [working] - End key -** to go to line end
**- [missing] - Control key + End key -** to put cursor to end of the TextField and scroll to it
**- [working] - Shift key + Home key -** to select Text between cursor and line start
**- [missing] - Control key + Shift key + Home key -** to select text between cursor and TextField start and scroll to start offset
**- [working] - Shift key + Home key -** to select Text between cursor and line start
**- [missing] - Control key + Shift key + End key -** to select text between cursor and TextField end and scroll to bottom offset


### Tested on
Platform - Windows
Flutter - 2.8.1, channel - stable

Regards,
Sagar
comments:
@sagarkardani 
Since Windows release has hit stable on 2.10, can you upgrade to 2.10.0 and try again to see if the missing ones listed are working or not ?

If not, can you just update your original post to show what's missing ?

Thanks.

@darshankawar

I just Updated flutter to 2.10.0

Below is the Updates. I am keeping only the missing ones...

### Consecutive mouse clicks for selection
- [missing] - Triple click to select the line/paragraph
- [missing] - Four click to select the whole TextField - This is not seen in each and every textinput, but it would be better if flutter adds this functionality.
- [missing] - Triple click or Four click to select the line/paragraph and then with click down and dragging the pointer, it keeps selecting the whole lines/paragraphs just by intersecting the bounds of lines/paragraphs. Also it autoscrolls when goes beyond boundary.

### Autoscroll while selection with mouse
- [missing] - While selecting and dragging the mouse pointer, whenever mouse pointer goes beyond the lower boundary or upper boundary, TextField should autoscroll and selection should proceed further in respective direction. At present scrolling works only when mouse pointer goes outside with click down and **kept moving around**. Usually scrolling should keep going even if just pointer is held outside boundary with click down. **It should not require moving around.**

### Autoscroll while selection with keyboard
- [missing] - While selecting and proceeding further with Shift key + Arrow keys, if selection goes beyond visible limit TextField should scroll. At present textselection is proceeding but **TextField is not scrolling in respective direction**.

### Combination keys
- [missing] - Control key + Shift key + Home key - to select text between cursor and TextField start and scroll to start offset - At present text is being selected but **TextField is not scrolling to start offset**.
- [missing] - Control key + Shift key + End key - to select text between cursor and TextField end and scroll to bottom offset - At present text is being selected but **TextField is not scrolling to end offset**.


### Tested on
Platform - Windows
Flutter - 2.10.0, channel - stable

@sagarkardani 
Thanks for the update. I verified all sections you listed out and below are my observations:

- Consecutive mouse clicks for selection:

Triple clicking removes the selected text that was selected upon double click.
Four clicking seems to select not all text but majority of it from the textfield.

Question:  Is this behavior already as expected on non-Flutter apps ?

- Autoscroll while selection with mouse:

Using below code sample, I tried using mouse pointer to scroll up and down which autoscrolls the text selection properly.

```

This behavior I can replicate. Using shift + arrow keys, the text which is visible in the textfield is selected but can't autoscroll down to select the entire text which is not visibile.

- Combination keys:

If you could confirm the behavior of first subsection that has a question for you, would be great and then accordingly we can put this issue forward.

I verified all these behaviors on latest master and stable.

Yes, it does autoscroll. But this works only when pointer is kept moving. My point here is if autoscroll works without pointer movement and scrolling momentum/speed is dependent on distance between outer boundary and pointer then it would be better.

issues.title: Add `bool validateOnLostFocus` to the `FormField` and `Form` class
issues.body:
## Use case

`Forms` and `FormField`have an `autoValidate` property. But if set to true it will validate on every keystroke/change which is annoying if you start typing e.g. an email address and with the first press the message `Not a valid email address is displayed` 
It would be much more user friendly if an autoValidate option existed that waits with validation till the user(focus) leaves the field when he has finished editing.

## Proposal

add a new property 

`bool validateOnLostFocus`

to the `Form` and `FormField` classes that allows this functionality
comments:
Sounds good. I've been also thinking about concept of changing the current type of the `autoValidate` prop from `bool` into some kind of "enum type". In such way you could define different triggers (even custom ones) when exactly the validation should be performed:

`autoValidate=null`
`autoValidate=ValidationRule.onChange`
`autoValidate=ValidationRule.onBlur`
`autoValidate=ValidationRule.customDefined` (e.g. onChange with some debounce)

Such change is unfortunately backward incompatible as opposite to yours proposal.
@vintage what would be the benefit of it? What would `onBlur` mean?
@HansMuller Why did you put the material label on this? Doesn't this also affect Cupertino?

@escamoteur The first benefit is as mentioned previously - possibility to define own rule on when to run the validation automatically. The second one is having single property which controls the behavior of auto validation. As for `onBlur` - that's the opposite of `onFocus`.
Ah sorry didn't get it in the first look. Yeah would make sense would also prevent to create contradictionary states
@escamoteur - Good point, I read TextFormField, but it says FormField. Removed the label.
If I create a PR, is there any chance that it will be merged? This is a feature I miss for a long time...
@HansMuller there shouldn't speak anything agains a PR, right?

@escamoteur @laterdayi I implemented onFocus and onBlur here and it worked fine. Would we continue with this proposal or would actually adding to Form and FormField be better, or even both proposals?
Proposal of #140538.

In this example in `onFocus` I told it to open the browser and if so it adds the text **Navigate to google.com** , and change the color.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/50643541/6fcd6d96-aacd-496e-af16-51a8e198cc22


In the second example it just changes color as soon as it is blurred.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/50643541/998bc31a-4e69-4b9b-9359-cfb6bd748833

@Mairramer   How do you do this and do you provide some test code

I feel like it would be better to add onBlur and onFocus to the text field

And still a onUnfocus or the validateOnLostFocus would really be helpful to validate automatically
Am 28. Dez. 2023, 02:38 +0100 schrieb laterdayi ***@***.***>:
> @Mairramer How do you do this and do you provide some test code
> I feel like it would be better to add onBlur and onFocus to the text field
> —
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub, or unsubscribe.
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.Message ID: ***@***.***>

@escamoteur  I need to analyze it more calmly, but I think I would need a small refactor on the Form and FormField. Because apparently the way they work today, implementing onUnfocus would be more work. Since it can be used with Dropdown, TimePicker, etc, and we need to ensure that it works normally.
I can implement it individually, but I need to use it on everyone.

@Mairramer   onBlur and onFouce are features that I've seen a lot of issues with and haven't been dealt with yet. Being such a good framework, it shouldn't even take a lot of trouble to implement simple out-of-focus focusing on the web. This approach is too outdated，Looking forward to making changes

@laterdayi I understand. I think it's difficult for PR to be approved. But anyway, I think you could create an abstraction for a CustomTextFormField and CustomTextFieldController, at least for the problem we had (similar to yours) you solved it by creating it based on that.
@Mairramer that would solve the case if I want to reacto on the unfocus of the whole form, but not that of single TextFormFields where I want the validation happen the moment a TextFormfield is left

@escamoteur I understood. I will try to create an abstraction in the `Form/FormField` layer for this. In the company I work at, we created several abstractions to solve this, since Flutter doesn't have them.

The current implementation of onUnfocus is not that useful. If you type a invalid email and unfocus it will display the error message. So far so good. Then you go back in the input and edit the value, since there is no unfocus event the error message will still be displayed.

From an UI perspective, the user hasn't done anything wrong, he has edited his value and his expectation is (rightly so) that the message should disappear. That is not currently the case.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/61395656-6527-4b9b-946d-394df2e5461b)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d093afb9-8606-45ea-adab-1f8c94dfeaa8)


What should be done:

1. Mode 1: Display failures on unfocus
With this option the error messages are only shown when unfocussing

2. Mode 2: Display failures after a delay
With this option the error message is shown after a delay (eg: 1 second).

@HansMuller Just checking.  Not sure why you added, the tag, "a:desktop" this only happens when running on a **WEB** project on Windows Desktop.  Best regards, Karl
@longtimedeveloper - good point; I'll change the label

I reproduced this issue and working on it. This is from mixed some problems.
- The VSCode stops due to the breakpoint from assertion, but the source file that the assertion failed is not opened. It can continue from debug actions, but it looks like freezed.
- The Clipboard History emulates Ctrl+V, but on Chrome, different from typed from keyboard by hand, the `KeyboardEvent.code` is empty, and `KeyboardEvent.location` is 0 (=no location).
  - The Shift, Control, Alt, Meta keys with no location is not mapped any keys, in [dev/tools/gen_keycodes/data/web_logical_location_mapping.json](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/19a380ffe79f4fa2bc23edf47a39151332f91c6b/dev/tools/gen_keycodes/data/web_logical_location_mapping.json#L29-L32), and the assert will fail. This is on that log.
  (#98460)
  - The `KeyboardEvent.code` is used as key of `_pressingRecords` in `engine lib/web_ui/lib/src/engine/keyboard_binding.dart`, and `_pressedKeys` in `packages/flutter/lib/src/services/hardware_keyboard.dart` to store pressed keys, but the `KeyboardEvent.code` of both emulated Ctrl and V are empty and the two will same key on `_pressedKey`. So, [the assert on hardware_keyboard.dart](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/4d9e6d9b3ea2c6d4b9c87a8ae3231961676a4b62/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/hardware_keyboard.dart#L444-L447) will fail.

issues.title: Moving cursor while composing should stay in composing region
comments:
### iOS and Android native behavior

The expected behavior above — keep the cursor inside the composing region — describes how both iOS and Android behave, modulo a couple of exceptions. In more detail:

* On iOS, using the long-press-on-space "floating cursor" feature, it always behaves this way — I've never succeeded in getting that feature to place the cursor outside the composing region. While you're dragging, the cursor will move fluidly in tandem with your finger (just like it will move outside the bounds of the whole text input); but when you release, it snaps to the nearest point within the composing region.

* On iOS, if you tap inside the text field before the start of the composing region, it always behaves this way, taking the cursor only to the start of the composing region.

  If you try repeatedly to repro this, it's easy to make a double-tap instead, which does something different. But if you wait a second between taps, you can tap before the start of the region many times in a row and the cursor stays inside the region.

* On iOS, if you tap after the end of the composing region, it usually behaves this way. Sometimes it instead places the cursor where you tapped and cancels composing, just like Flutter's current behavior.

  I haven't worked out a definite pattern of when it does so. Conceivably it's a bug, but probably it's some kind of design choice. Potentially we should imitate this wrinkle, once we figure out what the pattern is.

* On Android, with Gboard in Japanese (using the "12-key" kana keyboard), it always behaves this way. But it looks at first as if it doesn't:

  * When you tap outside the composing region, the cursor appears to go where you tapped: it starts being painted there. The composing region stays highlighted, though.

  * However, if you tap another kana key, the kana you typed doesn't appear where the cursor seemed to be. Instead it appears at the beginning or end of the composing region, according to whether you tried to put the cursor before or after the region.

    So the behavior shows that the cursor's real location is at the beginning or end of the composing region. There's a discrepancy in where the cursor is painted, but that seems like a bug in Android and/or Gboard.

  * Similarly if you hit delete, or use the arrow keys, the behavior shows that the cursor's real location was within the composing region, and it was just being painted in the wrong place.

* On Android, with Gboard in Chinese (using a pinyin keyboard), the UI is quite different — the pinyin letters don't appear in the text input at all, but instead just above the keyboard, and the text input only ever shows the completed input after composing. So this question doesn't come up.


In short, both iOS and Android always clamp the cursor to within the composing region when applicable, with two exceptions:
* Android/Gboard has a bug that affects where the cursor appears to be, but not its actual behavior.
* iOS has one case where it behaves differently.
